raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year> {$290.00} was fraudulently charge to 3 of my credit cards. AMEX ( 1 of the 3 ) has failed to complete any due diligence and attempted to make me responsible for the charges. 

I disputed the charge stating it was fraud, they did nothing. The stated it was a " chip '' charge and that I was responsible. From my initial notification I offered video and GPS data along with police and merchant case numbers to assist. I was told the fraud team would contact me. 

The AMEX Fraud team never contacted me, and AMEX said looks like this was done in person at the store, so I was responsible. 

I then again called and insisted to speak to the fraud team. I asked what investigation they did, answer of course, NONE! They simply validated the charge type. Then after I had to educate the fraud team that credit cards can be cloned and used in person, I was asked if I was sure I wanted the fraud to be investigated since it was an in-person charge and I still had possession of the card? 

I reminded AMEX yet again for the second or third time, I have XXXX XXXX footage and GPS evidence that I was over 8 miles away when the " in-person '' charge was made. 

Then at my insistence provided police report and store investigation numbers since they still seem to have no desire to do anything. 

This card charges {$590.00} as an annual fee, I'm a clear victim of fraud and they express no interest or willingness to investigate.
